public_status: BHSM v1.0.0 internal boundary no-fit package complete/exported; external empirical comparison layer separate/open

claim_statuses.boundary: BHSM v1.0.0 internal boundary no-fit package is complete/exported; external empirical comparison remains separate/open.
Requires boundary-derived chi; no mass fit is allowed.
Requires P_ch -> Tr_sector(P_ch) -> C_ch -> K_boundary[C_ch] before projection.
rho_ch=3 in local q^2+rho_ch*j^2 is not A-background.
Internal boundary predictions are exported; empirical comparison is a separate one-way test layer and remains open.
Local action-unit branch only; not numerical closure.
Identity collar dependency order is implemented; anisotropic chi remains open.

claim_statuses.boundary: This rejects the minimal diagonal collar route as primary, not BHSM.
Identity dependency-order scaffold only; chi remains open.

No gate was promoted without repo-derived source objects; empirical derivation inputs were not used.
tau and sigma are exported as part of the v1.0.0 internal boundary package; no empirical derivation inputs are used.

Gaussian/topographic normal form localized; Phi_0 is symbolic only until sigma, domain, and measure are derived.

Closed conditionally by CANONICAL_PROFILE_HESSIAN_THEOREM; no observed Higgs value is used.

claim_statuses.claim: Author-supplied internal Berger radius selection theorem
claim_statuses.boundary: Author BHSM axiom identifies r_internal_profile^2 = Lambda_squared = S = 1/(4*pi); this closes only the radius gate.
r_internal_profile = 1/sqrt(4*pi); tau remains blocked by Z_H and kappa_H.
Lambda-radius and overlap-radius are selected as semantically equivalent under the author normalization.
Profile normalization and Hessian closure are exported in the v1.0.0 internal package.
Author BHSM theorem identifies kappa_H with mu_H = 64*pi^5.
sigma=4*pi^(5/2), tau=1/(4*pi^(3/2)) from author-normalized boundary package.
Internal boundary no-fit package exported; external empirical comparison remains separate/open.
